content.title: The impact of climate change on butterflies in Britain

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: According to conservationists, populations of around two thirds of butterfly species have declined in Britain over the past 40 years. If this trend continues, it might have unpredictable knock-on effects for other species in the ecosystem. Butterfly eggs develop into caterpillars and these insects, which are the second stage in a new butterfly's life cycle, consume vast quantities of plant material, and in turn act as prey for birds as well as bats and other small mammals. Only by arming themselves with an understanding of why butterfly numbers are down can conservationists hope to halt or reverse the decline.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Butterflies prefer outdoor conditions to be "just right", which means neither too hot nor too cold. Under the conditions of climate change, the temperature at any given time in summer is generally getting warmer, leaving butterflies with the challenge of how to deal with this. One of the main ways in which species are ensuring conditions suit them is by changing the time of year at which they are active and reproduce. Scientists refer to the timing of such life cycle events as "phenology", so when an animal or plant starts to do something earlier in the year than it usually does, it is said to be "advancing its phenology".

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: These advances have been observed already in a wide range of butterflies - indeed, most species are advancing their phenology to some extent. In Britain, as the average spring temperature has increased by roughly 0.5 °C over the past 20 years, species have advanced by between three days and a week on average, to keep in line with cooler temperatures. Is this a sign that butterflies are well equipped to cope with climate change, and readily adjust to new temperatures? Or are these populations under stress, being dragged along unwillingly by unnaturally fast changes? The answer is still unknown, but a new study is seeking to answer these questions.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: First, the researchers pulled together data from millions of records that had been submitted by butterfly enthusiasts - people who spend their free time observing the activities of different species. This provided information on 130 species of butterflies in Great Britain every year for a 20-year period. They then estimated the abundance and distribution of each species across this time, along with how far north in the country they had moved. The data also, crucially, allowed researchers to estimate subtle changes in what time of the year each species was changing into an adult butterfly.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Analysing the trends in each variable, the researchers discovered that species with more flexible life cycles were more likely to be able to benefit from an earlier emergence driven by climate change. Some species are able to go from caterpillar to butterfly twice or more per year, so that the individual butterflies you see flying in the spring are the grandchildren or great-grandchildren of the individuals seen a year previously.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Among these species, researchers observed that those which have been advancing their phenology the most over the 20-year study period also had the most positive trends in abundance, distribution and northwards extent. For these species, such as Britain's tiniest butterfly, the dainty Small Blue, whose colonies are up to a hundred strong, some develop into butterflies early in spring, allowing their summer generations to complete another reproductive cycle by autumn so that more population growth occurs.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Other species, however, are less flexible and restricted to a single reproductive cycle per year. For these species, there was no evidence of any benefit to emerging earlier. Indeed, worryingly, it was found that the species in this group that specialise in very specific habitat types, often related to the caterpillar's preferred diet, actually tended to be most at harm from advancing phenology. The beautiful High Brown Fritillary, often described as Britain's most endangered butterfly, is in this group. It is found only in coppiced woodland and limestone pavement habitats. It is also a single-generation butterfly that has advanced its phenology. This suggests that climate change, while undoubtedly not the sole cause, might have played a part in the downfall of this species.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: All is not lost, however. Many of Britain's single-generation species show the capacity, in continental Europe, to add a second generation in years that are sufficiently warm. Therefore, as the climate continues to warm, species like the Silver-studded Blue might be able to switch to multiple generations in the UK as well, and so begin to extract benefits from the additional warmth, potentially leading to population increases.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: More immediately, conservationists can arm themselves with all this knowledge to spot the warning signs of species that may be at risk. The White Admiral of southern England, a much sought-after butterfly, experienced a significant increase in numbers from the 1920s but has shown a considerable decline in the past 20 years. This may be because the caterpillar exists solely on a diet of a plant called honeysuckle. But it is also likely to be due to climate change.

groups.desc:
Questions 1-6
Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 1-6 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

questions.title: Forty years ago, there were fewer butterflies in Britain than at present.
questions.analysis: 这个答案是正确的，因为题目询问的是40年前英国的蝴蝶数量是否比现在少。文章中直接提供了相关证据，说明在过去的40年间，英国约三分之二的蝴蝶种群数量已经下降了。这意味着40年前蝴蝶的数量实际上比现在多，直接与题干表述相反。因此，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: According to conservationists, populations of around two thirds of butterfly species have declined in Britain over the past 40 years.
central_sentences.translation: 据保护主义者说，在过去的40年里，英国约三分之二的蝴蝶种群数量已经下降。

target_sen.target_sen: According to conservationists, populations of around two thirds of butterfly species have declined in Britain over the past 40 years.
According to conservationists, populations of around two thirds of butterfly species have declined in Britain over the past 40 years.

questions.title: Caterpillars are eaten by a number of different predators.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Caterpillars are eaten by a number of different predators." 与原文中的 "caterpillars... act as prey for birds as well as bats and other small mammals" 是同义表达。原文明确指出毛毛虫是鸟类、蝙蝠和其他小型哺乳动物的猎物，这与题干中的 "eaten by a number of different predators" 完全一致。因此，答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Butterfly eggs develop into caterpillars and these insects, which are the second stage in a new butterfly's life cycle, consume vast quantities of plant material, and in turn act as prey for birds as well as bats and other small mammals.
central_sentences.translation: 蝴蝶卵发育成毛毛虫，这些昆虫是新蝴蝶生命周期的第二阶段，消耗大量植物材料，并且反过来成为鸟类、蝙蝠和其他小型哺乳动物的猎物。

target_sen.target_sen: Butterfly eggs develop into caterpillars and these insects, which are the second stage in a new butterfly's life cycle, consume vast quantities of plant material, and in turn act as prey for birds as well as bats and other small mammals.

Butterfly eggs develop into caterpillars and these insects, which are the second stage in a new butterfly's life cycle, consume vast quantities of plant material, and in turn act as prey for birds as well as bats and other small mammals.

Butterfly eggs develop into caterpillars and these insects, which are the second stage in a new butterfly's life cycle, consume vast quantities of plant material, and in turn act as prey for birds as well as bats and other small mammals.

Butterfly eggs develop into caterpillars and these insects, which are the second stage in a new butterfly's life cycle, consume vast quantities of plant material, and in turn act as prey for birds as well as bats and other small mammals.

Butterfly eggs develop into caterpillars and these insects, which are the second stage in a new butterfly's life cycle, consume vast quantities of plant material, and in turn act as prey for birds as well as bats and other small mammals.

questions.title: 'Phenology' is a term used to describe a creature's ability to alter the location of a life cycle event.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息错误地将phenology与位置（location）联系在一起，而原文明确指出phenology是与时间（timing）相关。因此，题干中的描述与原文不符，答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Scientists refer to the timing of such life cycle events as "phenology", so when an animal or plant starts to do something earlier in the year than it usually does, it is said to be "advancing its phenology".
central_sentences.translation: 科学家将这种生命周期事件的时间安排称为“phenology”，因此当动物或植物在一年中的某个时间比通常更早开始做某事时，就被称为“提前其phenology”。

target_sen.target_sen: Scientists refer to the timing of such life cycle events as "phenology", so when an animal or plant starts to do something earlier in the year than it usually does, it is said to be "advancing its phenology".

questions.title: Some species of butterfly have a reduced lifespan due to spring temperature increases.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Some species of butterfly have a reduced lifespan due to spring temperature increases” 在文章中没有明确提及。虽然文章讨论了春季气温升高和蝴蝶现象的提前（phenology advancing），但这并不同于寿命的减少。“Reduced lifespan”（寿命缩短）这个概念在文章中完全没有出现，因此正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”（未提及）。学生需要注意的是，题目中的线索词“reduced lifespan”必须要在原文中找到明确的同义替换，但此概念文章中未曾提及，故答案是“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.reason: 未提及 lifespan 或寿命相关变化
central_sentences.analysis: 原文未提及寿命缩短，只谈出现时间提前。

questions.title: There is a clear reason for the adaptations that butterflies are making to climate change.
questions.analysis: 本题的题干是“There is a clear reason for the adaptations that butterflies are making to climate change.”，其中的线索词包括“clear reason”, “adaptations”, “butterflies”, 和 “climate change”。在原文中，同义替换词“clear reason”直接对应的短语没有说明一个明确的原因，而“adaptations”以及“butterflies”和“climate change”被文章中多次提到和解释。当研究人员提到“答案仍然未知”，暗示他们尚未找到一个清晰的原因。因此，题干表述和文章内容不符，应该回答“FALSE”。

central_sentences.sentence: Is this a sign that butterflies are well equipped to cope with climate change, and readily adjust to new temperatures? Or are these populations under stress, being dragged along unwillingly by unnaturally fast changes? The answer is still unknown, but a new study is seeking to answer these questions.
central_sentences.translation: 这是否意味着蝴蝶能很好地应对气候变化，并轻易适应新温度？还是这些群体在压力下被不自然的快速变化拖累？答案仍然未知，但一项新研究正在努力回答这些问题。

target_sen.target_sen: These advances have been observed already in a wide range of butterflies - indeed, most species are advancing their phenology to some extent.
Is this a sign that butterflies are well equipped to cope with climate change, and readily adjust to new temperatures?
Is this a sign that butterflies are well equipped to cope with climate change, and readily adjust to new temperatures?

questions.title: The data used in the study was taken from the work of amateur butterfly watchers.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“data”和“amateur butterfly watchers”在原文中分别对应“records”和“butterfly enthusiasts”。原文明确提到研究人员使用了由蝴蝶爱好者提交的数据，因此题干中的信息在文章中可以找到对应，答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: First, the researchers pulled together data from millions of records that had been submitted by butterfly enthusiasts - people who spend their free time observing the activities of different species.
central_sentences.translation: 首先，研究人员汇集了由蝴蝶爱好者提交的数百万条记录数据——这些人利用空闲时间观察不同物种的活动。

target_sen.target_sen: First, the researchers pulled together data from millions of records that had been submitted by butterfly enthusiasts - people who spend their free time observing the activities of different species.
First, the researchers pulled together data from millions of records that had been submitted by butterfly enthusiasts - people who spend their free time observing the activities of different species.

groups.title: Butterflies in the UK
groups.desc:
Questions 7-13
Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 7-13 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect: The Small Blue • lives in large $34125$ • first appears at the start of $34126$ • completes more than one reproductive cycle per year The High Brown Fritillary • has one reproductive cycle • is considered to be more $34127$ than other species • its caterpillars occupy a limited range of $34128$ The Silver-studded Blue • is already able to reproduce twice a year in warm areas of $34129$ The White Admiral • is found in $34130$ areas of England • both climate change and the $34131$ of the caterpillar are possible reasons for decline

questions.analysis: 题干中的“lives in large”对应原文中的“whose colonies are up to a hundred strong”。“colonies”指的是群体，且数量多达上百只，符合题干中“large”的描述。因此，答案是“colonies”。

central_sentences.sentence: For these species, such as Britain's tiniest butterfly, the dainty Small Blue, whose colonies are up to a hundred strong, some develop into butterflies early in spring, allowing their summer generations to complete another reproductive cycle by autumn so that more population growth occurs.
central_sentences.translation: 对于这些物种，例如英国最小的蝴蝶——娇小的蓝小蝶，其群体多达上百只，有些在春天早早变成蝴蝶，使得夏季的世代能够在秋天前完成另一个繁殖周期，从而增加了种群数量。

target_sen.target_sen: For these species, such as Britain's tiniest butterfly, the dainty Small Blue, whose colonies are up to a hundred strong, some develop into butterflies early in spring, allowing their summer generations to complete another reproductive cycle by autumn so that more population growth occurs.

For these species, such as Britain's tiniest butterfly, the dainty Small Blue, whose colonies are up to a hundred strong, some develop into butterflies early in spring, allowing their summer generations to complete another reproductive cycle by autumn so that more population growth occurs.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "first appears at the start of" 意思是“在某个时间的开始首次出现”，而在文章中，"develop into butterflies early in spring" 表示“在春天早期变成蝴蝶”，这和题干中的描述是完全对应的。因此，正确答案是"spring"。

central_sentences.sentence: some develop into butterflies early in spring
central_sentences.translation: 一些在春天早期就变成蝴蝶。

target_sen.target_sen: For these species, such as Britain's tiniest butterfly, the dainty Small Blue, whose colonies are up to a hundred strong, some develop into butterflies early in spring, allowing their summer generations to complete another reproductive cycle by autumn so that more population growth occurs.

For these species, such as Britain's tiniest butterfly, the dainty Small Blue, whose colonies are up to a hundred strong, some develop into butterflies early in spring, allowing their summer generations to complete another reproductive cycle by autumn so that more population growth occurs.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“more 【 】 than other species”要求填入一个形容词，表示某个物种比其他物种更【 】。在文章中，明确提到“High Brown Fritillary”是“Britain's most endangered butterfly”，即“英国最濒危的蝴蝶”。这里的“most endangered”是一个比较级的描述，表示它比其他物种更濒危。因此，正确答案是“endangered”。

central_sentences.sentence: The beautiful High Brown Fritillary, often described as Britain's most endangered butterfly, is in this group.
central_sentences.translation: 美丽的高褐斑蝶，经常被描述为英国最濒危的蝴蝶，属于这一类。

target_sen.target_sen: Indeed, worryingly, it was found that the species in this group that specialise in very specific habitat types, often related to the caterpillar's preferred diet, actually tended to be most at harm from advancing phenology.
The beautiful High Brown Fritillary, often described as Britain's most endangered butterfly, is in this group.

questions.analysis: 题目中的句子 "its caterpillars occupy a limited range of 【 】" 中的 "occupy" 和 "a limited range of" 是答题关键的线索词。原文中对于这些表达的同义替换词是 "specialise in" 和 "very specific"。 因此，线索很明显地指向了原文中的 "specialise in very specific habitat types" 这一部分，由此得出答案为 "habitat" 或 "habitats"。这个答案是正确的，因为它准确地对应了题干中表达的毛毛虫生存范围受限的概念。

central_sentences.sentence: Indeed, worryingly, it was found that the species in this group that specialise in very specific habitat types, often related to the caterpillar's preferred diet, actually tended to be most at harm from advancing phenology.
central_sentences.translation: 实际上，令人担忧的是，研究发现，这组中的物种专注于非常特定的生境类型，通常与毛毛虫的首选食物有关，其实往往受到现象提前的最大伤害。

target_sen.target_sen: Indeed, worryingly, it was found that the species in this group that specialise in very specific habitat types, often related to the caterpillar's preferred diet, actually tended to be most at harm from advancing phenology.

Indeed, worryingly, it was found that the species in this group that specialise in very specific habitat types, often related to the caterpillar's preferred diet, actually tended to be most at harm from advancing phenology.

questions.analysis: 答案为 "Europe" 是因为题干中的 "reproduce twice a year" 对应文章中的 "add a second generation"；"warm areas" 对应文章中的 "sufficiently warm"。并且题干中的问句要求填写的是一个特定地域，即 "Europe"。题目要求填入一个词，而且文中明确指出是在 "continental Europe" 可以再生育一代，且条件是足够暖和。因此答案选择"Europe"。

central_sentences.sentence: Many of Britain's single-generation species show the capacity, in continental Europe, to add a second generation in years that are sufficiently warm.
central_sentences.translation: 英国的许多单代种类显示出在欧洲大陆，在足够温暖的年份中增加第二代的能力。

target_sen.target_sen: Many of Britain's single-generation species show the capacity, in continental Europe, to add a second generation in years that are sufficiently warm.
Many of Britain's single-generation species show the capacity, in continental Europe, to add a second generation in years that are sufficiently warm.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“is found in ... areas of England”对应原文中的“The White Admiral of southern England”。从中我们可以确定“White Admiral”蝴蝶是在英格兰的“southern”地区被发现。因此，答案是“southern”。

central_sentences.sentence: The White Admiral of southern England, a much sought-after butterfly, experienced a significant increase in numbers from the 1920s but has shown a considerable decline in the past 20 years.
central_sentences.translation: 英格兰南部的白舰长蝴蝶，（过去）一直是非常受欢迎的蝴蝶，其数量自20世纪20年代大幅增加，但在过去20年中出现了显著的下降。

questions.analysis: 我们在题干中找到了线索词"climate change"和"caterpillar"，并且题干的结构明确要求填入的是导致某物种数量下降的原因之一。通过在原文中搜索这些线索词，我们锁定了一句结束为"due to climate change"的句子，而该句前半部分明确提到毛毛虫的食物(diet)，这与题干的要求完全一致。因此，答案是“diet”。

central_sentences.sentence: This may be because the caterpillar exists solely on a diet of a plant called honeysuckle. But it is also likely to be due to climate change.
central_sentences.translation: 这可能是因为毛毛虫仅以一种叫做忍冬植物为食。但这也可能是因为气候变化。

target_sen.target_sen: But it is also likely to be due to climate change.
This may be because the caterpillar exists solely on a diet of a plant called honeysuckle.
But it is also likely to be due to climate change.
This may be because the caterpillar exists solely on a diet of a plant called honeysuckle.

content.title: Deep-sea mining
content.summary: {'A': '马普顿教授发现深海海绵中的微生物能杀死致病细菌，其所含抗生素有成为救命药的潜力，但他和其他科学家担心深海采矿会使这种潜力丧失。', 'B': '普利茅斯大学的医学微生物学家厄普顿呼吁停止深海采矿以权衡利弊，海洋学家已发现许多新物种可能有救命潜力，而新抗生素成为药物可能需要十年，商业采矿已开始。', 'C': '美国地质调查局称深海含有的镍、钴和稀土金属比陆地储备总和还多，矿业公司认为深海采矿可帮助金属供应多样化且优于陆地采矿。', 'D': '环境和法律团体呼吁谨慎，称深海采矿对环境和周边社区可能有巨大未知影响，全球监管框架尚未起草。', 'E': '科学研究表明深海海床和热液喷口对生物多样性和全球气候有重要影响，深海采矿会对其构成严重威胁。', 'F': '加拿大水下勘探公司首席执行官迈克·约翰斯顿认为应环保地探索海洋未开发潜力，而批评者称深海奇特且鲜为人知的生态系统可能被破坏需保护。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: When Professor Mat Upton found that a microbe from a deep-sea sponge was killing pathogenic bugs in his laboratory, he realised it could be a breakthrough in the fight against antibiotic-resistant superbugs, which are responsible for thousands of deaths a year in the UK alone. Further tests confirmed that an antibiotic from the sponge bacteria, found living more than 700 metres under the sea at the Rockall Trough in the north-east Atlantic, was previously unknown to science, boosting its potential as a life-saving medicine. But Upton, and other scientists who view the deep ocean and its wealth of unique and undocumented species as a prospecting ground for new medicines, fear such potential will be lost in the rush to exploit the deep sea's equally rich metal and mineral resources.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: "We're looking at the bioactive potential of marine resources, to see if there are any more medicines or drugs down there before we destroy it for ever," says Upton, a medical microbiologist at the University of Plymouth. He is among many scientists urging a halt to deep-sea mining, asking for time to weigh up the pros and cons. "In sustainability terms, this could be a better way of exploiting the economic potential of the deep sea," he argues. Oceanographers using remotely operated vehicles have spotted many new species. Among them have been sea cucumbers with tails allowing them to sail along the ocean floor, and a rare "Dumbo" octopus, found 3,000 metres under the Pacific Ocean, off the coast of California. Any one of these could offer lifesaving potential. Upton estimates it could take up to a decade for a newly discovered antibiotic to become a medicine—but the race towards commercial mining in the ocean abyss has already begun.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The deep sea contains more nickel, cobalt and rare earth metals than all land reserves combined, according to the US Geological Survey. Mining corporations argue that deep-sea exploration could help diversify the supply of metals and point to the fact that demand for resources such as copper, aluminium, cobalt for electric car batteries and other metals to power technology and smartphones is soaring. They say that deep-sea mining could yield far superior ore to land mining with little, if any, waste. Different methods of extraction exist, but most involve employing some form of converted machinery previously used in terrestrial mining to excavate materials from the sea floor, at depths of up to 6,000 metres, then drawing a seawater slurry, containing rock and other solid particles, from the sea floor to ships on the surface. The slurry is then "de-watered" and transferred to another vessel for shipping. Extracted seawater is pumped back down and discharged close to the sea floor.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: But environmental and legal groups have urged caution, arguing there are potentially massive and unknown ramifications for the environment and for nearby communities, and that the global regulatory framework is not yet drafted. "Despite arising in the last half century, the ‘new global gold rush’ of deep-sea mining shares many features with past resource scrambles—including a general disregard for environmental and social impacts, and the marginalisation of indigenous peoples and their rights," a paper, written by Julie Hunter and Julian Aguon, from Blue Ocean Law, and Pradeep Singh, from the Center for Marine Environmental Sciences, Bremen, argues. The authors say that knowledge of the deep seabed remains extremely limited. "The surface of the Moon, Mars and even Venus have all been mapped and studied in much greater detail, leading marine scientists to commonly remark that, with respect to the deep sea, ‘We don't yet know what we need to know.’

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Scientific research—including a recent paper in Marine Policy journal—has suggested the deep seabed, and hydrothermal vents, which are created when seawater meets volcanic magma, have crucial impacts upon biodiversity and the global climate. The mineral-rich vents and their surrounds are also home to many well-known animals including crustaceans, tubeworms, clams, slugs, anemones and fish. "It is becoming increasingly clear that deep-sea mining poses a grave threat to these vital seabed functions," the paper says. "Extraction methods would produce large sediment plumes and involve the discharge of waste back into the ocean, significantly disturbing seafloor environments," the paper continues. "On deep-sea vents, scientists are clear," says Dr Jon Copley of the National Oceanography Centre, Southampton: "we don't want mining on them."

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The oceans occupy around 70% of the planet and are relatively unexplored, says Mike Johnston, chief executive of Nautilus, a Canadian underwater exploration company: "It makes sense to explore this untapped potential in an environmentally sustainable way, instead of continually looking at the fast-depleting land resources of the planet to meet society's rising needs." Those leading the global rush to place giant mining machines thousands of metres below the sea surface say the environmental impacts will be far lower than on land. But critics say exotic and little-known ecosystems in the deep oceans could be destroyed and must be protected. "Mining will be the greatest assault on deep-sea ecosystems ever inflicted by humans," according to hydrothermal vent expert Verena Tunnicliffe, at the University of Victoria in Canada. She argues that active vents must be off-limits for mining to protect the new knowledge and biotechnology spin-offs they can deliver, and that strict controls must be in place elsewhere.

groups.desc:
Questions 14-17
Reading Passage 2 has six paragraphs, A-F.
Which paragraph contains the following information?
Write the correct letter, A-F, in boxes 14-17 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: reference to the rapidly increasing need for one raw material in the transport industry
questions.analysis: 本题目要求找出某种原材料在运输行业中需求迅速增加的参考。题干中的"rapidly increasing need"在段落C中用"soaring demand"表示，而"one raw material in the transport industry"则对应"cobalt for electric car batteries"。原文中的这句话准确表达了某种原材料（钴）在运输行业（电动车）中需求迅速增加的观点，因此段落C是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Mining corporations argue that deep-sea exploration could help diversify the supply of metals and point to the fact that demand for resources such as copper, aluminium, cobalt for electric car batteries and other metals to power technology and smartphones, is soaring.
central_sentences.translation: 矿业公司认为，深海勘探可以帮助多样化金属供应，并指出铜、铝、用于电动车电池的钴和其他用于技术和智能手机的金属的需求正在飞速增长。

target_sen.target_sen: Mining corporations argue that deep-sea exploration could help diversify the supply of metals and point to the fact that demand for resources such as copper, aluminium, cobalt for electric car batteries and other metals to power technology and smartphones, is soaring.

questions.title: a rough estimate of the area of the Earth covered by the oceans
questions.analysis:
本题句子段落信息配对题中，线索词为 "rough estimate" 和 "Earth covered by the oceans"。
 这些线索词在段落 F 中的 "occupy around" 和 "occupy the planet" 中得到了对应。通过这些关键短语，我们可以定位到段落 F。
 段落 F 提供了一个具体的数据："The oceans occupy around 70% of the planet"，明确说明了地球被海洋覆盖的面积，这是其他段落所不具备的。因此，段落 F 是唯一准确的选择。

central_sentences.sentence: The oceans occupy around 70% of the planet and are relatively unexplored, says Mike Johnston
central_sentences.translation: 海洋占地球大约 70%，并且相对未被探索，迈克·约翰斯顿说道。

target_sen.target_sen: The oceans occupy around 70% of the planet and are relatively unexplored, says Mike Johnston, chief executive of Nautilus, a Canadian underwater exploration company: "It makes sense to explore this untapped potential in an environmentally sustainable way, instead of continually looking at the fast depleting land resources of the planet to meet society's rising needs."

questions.title: how a particular underwater habitat, where minerals and organisms co-exist, is formed
questions.analysis: 根据题目中的关键词“underwater habitat”、“minerals”、“organisms”和“formed”，可以推断我们需要找到一个深海环境，该环境中矿物质和生物共存，并且描述了它是如何形成的。段落E详细描述了深海底床和热液喷口的形成过程，以及它们如何对生物多样性产生影响，这些内容与题目所问的内容完全匹配，因此正确答案是段落E。

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "worthwhile" 对应原文中的 "better way"，"extract" 对应 "exploiting"，"minerals" 对应 "metal and mineral resources"。通过这些同义替换词，可以确定 Mat Upton 强调了海洋资源中的药用价值，而不仅仅是矿物资源。因此，正确答案是 A. Professor Mat Upton。

questions.analysis: 正确答案是E.Verena Tunnicliffe。在文章Section F中，Verena Tunnicliffe明确提到深海采矿将是人类对深海生态系统的最大攻击，这与题干中提到的“destructive impact on marine life”是同义的表达，直接对应了题干的线索词。

central_sentences.sentence: "Mining will be the greatest assault on deep-sea ecosystems ever inflicted by humans," according to hydrothermal vent expert Verena Tunnicliffe, at the University of Victoria in Canada.

groups.title: Mining the sea floor
groups.desc:
Questions 24-26
Complete the summary below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 24-26 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect:
Mining corporations believe that the mineral resources lying under the sea may be superior to those found in the earth. They also say that these can be removed without producing much $34142$.

The extraction is often done by adapting the $34143$ that has already been used to work on land. The method of excavation involves removing the seawater from the slurry that is brought up to ships and returning it to the seabed. However, concerned groups strongly believe that $34144$ is necessary due to the possible number of unidentified consequences.

questions.analysis: 通过题干和原文中的同义表达对应关系：题干中的“removed”对应原文中的“yield”，“without producing much”对应“with little, if any”。结合这些对应关系，可以确定答案句中的“waste”就是题干所需要填的单词。因此，答案是“waste”。

central_sentences.sentence: They say that deep-sea mining could yield far superior ore to land mining with little, if any, waste.
central_sentences.translation: 他们说，深海采矿可以产生比陆地采矿优质得多的矿石，而且几乎没有废物。

target_sen.target_sen: They say that deep-sea mining could yield far superior ore to land mining with little, if any, waste.
They say that deep-sea mining could yield far superior ore to land mining with little, if any, waste.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是"machinery"，因为题干中的"adapting the... that has already been used to work on land"在文章中直接对应的是"converted machinery previously used in terrestrial mining"。两者中的关键词分别是"adapting"和"converted"，以及"used to work on land"和"previously used in terrestrial mining"，答案句中的"machinery"符合题干词汇的指代，因此为正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Different methods of extraction exist, but most involve employing some form of converted machinery previously used in terrestrial mining to excavate materials from the sea floor, at
central_sentences.translation: 不同的提取方法存在，但大多数都涉及到使用某种以前用于陆地采矿的改装机械在海底挖掘材料...

target_sen.target_sen: Different methods of extraction exist, but most involve employing some form of converted machinery previously used in terrestrial mining to excavate materials from the sea floor, at depths of up to 6,000 metres, then drawing a seawater slurry, containing rock and other solid particles, from the sea floor to ships on the surface.

Different methods of extraction exist, but most involve employing some form of converted machinery previously used in terrestrial mining to excavate materials from the sea floor, at depths of up to 6,000 metres, then drawing a seawater slurry, containing rock and other solid particles, from the sea floor to ships on the surface.

questions.analysis: 答案是“caution”，因为题干中的“concerned groups strongly believe that caution is necessary due to the possible number of unidentified consequences”在原文中对应的句子是“environmental and legal groups have urged caution, arguing there are potentially massive and unknown ramifications”。通过对比题干和原文，可以发现“concerned groups”对应“environmental and legal groups”，“strongly believe”对应“have urged”，“necessary”对应“urged”，“unidentified consequences”对应“potentially massive and unknown ramifications”。因此，答案是“caution”。

central_sentences.sentence: But environmental and legal groups have urged caution, arguing there are potentially massive and unknown ramifications for the environment and for nearby communities, and that the global regulatory framework is not yet drafted.
central_sentences.translation: 但是，环境和法律团体敦促谨慎，认为对环境和附近社区可能会有巨大的未知影响，并且全球监管框架尚未制定。

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: There has long been a general assumption that human beings are essentially selfish. We're apparently ruthless, with strong impulses to compete against each other for resources and to accumulate power and possessions. If we are kind to one another, it's usually because we have ulterior motives. If we are good, it's only because we have managed to control and transcend our innate selfishness and brutality.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: This bleak view of human nature is closely associated with the science writer Richard Dawkins, whose 1976 book The Selfish Gene became popular because it fitted so well with—and helped to justify—the competitive and individualistic ethos that was so prevalent in late 20th-century societies. Like many others, Dawkins justifies his views with reference to the field of evolutionary psychology. Evolutionary psychology theorises that present-day human traits developed in prehistoric times, during what is termed the "environment of evolutionary adaptedness".

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Prehistory is usually seen as a period of intense competition, when life was such a brutal battle that only those with traits such as selfishness, aggression and ruthlessness survived. And because survival depended on access to resources—such as rivers, forests and animals—there was bound to be conflict between rival groups, which led to the development of traits such as racism and warfare. This seems logical. But, in fact, the assumption on which this all rests—that prehistoric life was a desperate struggle for survival—is false.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: It's important to remember that in the prehistoric era, the world was very sparsely populated. According to some estimates, around 15,000 years ago, the population of Europe was only 29,000, and the population of the whole world was less than half a million. Humans at that time were hunter-gatherers: people who lived by hunting wild animals and collecting wild plants. With such small population densities, it seems unlikely that prehistoric hunter-gatherer groups had to compete against each other for resources or had any need to develop ruthlessness and competitiveness, or to go to war.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: There is significant evidence to back this notion from contemporary hunter-gatherer groups, who live in the same way as prehistoric humans did. As the anthropologist Bruce Knauft has remarked, hunter-gatherers are characterised by "extreme political and sexual egalitarianism". Knauft has observed that individuals in such groups don't accumulate property or possessions and have an ethical obligation to share everything. They also have methods of preserving egalitarianism by ensuring that disparities of status don't arise.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The !Kung people of southern Africa, for example, swap arrows before going hunting and when an animal is killed, the acclaim does not go to the person who fired the arrow, but to the person the arrow belongs to. And if a person becomes too domineering, the other members of the group ostracise them, exiling the offender from society. Typically in such groups, men do not dictate what women do. Women in hunter-gatherer groups worldwide often benefit from a high level of autonomy, being able to select their own marriage partners, decide what work they do and work whenever they choose to. And if a marriage breaks down, they have custody rights over their children.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Many anthropologists believe that societies such as the !Kung were normal until a few thousand years ago, when population growth led to the development of agriculture and a settled lifestyle. In view of the above, there seems little reason to assume that traits such as racism, warfare and male domination should have been selected by evolution—as they would have been of little benefit in the prehistoric era. Individuals who behaved selfishly and ruthlessly would be less likely to survive, since they would have been ostracised from their groups.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: It makes more sense, then, to see traits such as cooperation, egalitarianism, altruism and peacefulness as innate characteristics of human beings. These were the traits that were prevalent in human life for tens of thousands of years. So presumably these traits are still strong in us now.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: But if prehistoric life wasn't really as brutal as has often been assumed, why do modern humans behave so selfishly and ruthlessly? Perhaps these negative traits should be seen as a later development, the result of environmental and psychological factors. Research has shown repeatedly that when the natural habitats of primates such as apes and gorillas are disrupted, they tend to become more violent and hierarchical.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: So, it could well be that the same thing has happened to us. I believe that the end of the hunter-gatherer lifestyle and the advent of farming was connected to a psychological change that occurred in some groups of people. There was a new sense of individuality and separateness, which led to a new selfishness, and ultimately to hierarchical societies, patriarchy and warfare. At any rate, these negative traits appear to have developed so recently that it doesn't seem feasible to explain them in adaptive or evolutionary terms.

describing a commonly held belief about people's behaviour
explaining why he thinks that humans act in a selfish manner

questions.analysis: 题干中的“commonly held belief”和文章中的“a general assumption”是同义替换，表明这是一个普遍的观点。所以正确答案应该是描述这种普遍观点。而文章确实在描述人类行为的普遍信念，因此选项C是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: There has long been a general assumption that human beings are essentially selfish.
central_sentences.translation: 长期以来，人们普遍认为人类本质上是自私的。

questions.title: What point is made about Richard Dawkins' book The Selfish Gene?

options.text: Its appeal lay in the radical nature of its ideas.
Its success was due to the scientific support it offered.
It presented a view that was in line with the attitudes of its time.
It took an innovative approach to the analysis of human psychology.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为文章明确指出《自私的基因》之所以流行，是因为它非常符合并帮助证明了20世纪后期社会中普遍存在的竞争和个人主义精神。这与选项C中的“in line with the attitudes of its time”是同义表达。

central_sentences.sentence: This bleak view of human nature is closely associated with the science writer Richard Dawkins, whose 1976 book The Selfish Gene became popular because it fitted so well with - and helped to justify - the competitive and individualistic ethos that was so prevalent in late 20th-century societies.
central_sentences.translation: 这种对人性悲观的看法与科学作家理查德·道金斯密切相关，他的1976年出版的《自私的基因》之所以流行，是因为它非常符合并帮助证明了20世纪后期社会中普遍存在的竞争和个人主义精神。

questions.title: What does the writer suggest about the prehistoric era in the fourth paragraph?

options.text: Societies were more complex than many people believe.
Supplies of natural resources were probably relatively plentiful.
Most estimates about population sizes are likely to be inaccurate.
Humans moved across continents more than was previously thought.

questions.analysis: 本题主要是关于史前时代资源充足与否的讨论。文章指出，由于那时人口密度小，狩猎采集群体不需要为了资源竞争，这隐含了资源是相对充足的。因此选项B是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: With such small population densities, it seems unlikely that prehistoric hunter-gatherer groups had to compete against each other for resources or had any need to develop ruthlessness and competitiveness, or to go to war.
central_sentences.translation: 由于这种低人口密度，史前的狩猎采集群体似乎不太可能为了资源而相互竞争，或有发展残忍和竞争性，或去打仗的需要。

questions.title: The writer refers to Bruce Knauft's work as support for the idea that

options.text: selfishness is a relatively recent development in human societies.
only people in isolated communities can live in an unselfish manner.
very few lifestyles have survived unchanged since prehistoric times.
hunter-gatherer cultures worldwide are declining in number.

questions.analysis: Bruce Knauft的研究表明，原始社会中的人类群体表现出极端的平等主义和共享精神，这与现代社会中的自私行为形成鲜明对比。因此，选项A“selfishness is a relatively recent development in human societies”是正确的，因为它表明自私是人类社会中相对较新的发展。

central_sentences.sentence: Knauft has observed that individuals in such groups don"t accumulate property or possessions and have an ethical obligation to share everything. They
central_sentences.translation: Knauft观察到，这些群体中的个人不会积累财产或物品，并且有道德义务分享一切。

target_sen.target_sen: As the anthropologist Bruce Knauft has remarked, hunter-gatherers are characterised by "extreme political and sexual egalitarianism".
Knauft has observed that individuals in such groups don"t accumulate property or possessions and have an ethical obligation to share everything.

groups.title: Contemporary hunter-gatherer societies
groups.desc:
Questions 31-35
Complete the summary below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 31-35 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect: Bruce Knauft's research shows that contemporary hunter-gatherer societies tend to exhibit a high level of $34149$ in all areas of life. In these cultures, distributing resources fairly among all members is a moral obligation. These societies also employ strategies to prevent differences in $34150$ occurring: for example, the !Kung follow a custom whereby the credit for one person's success at $34151$ is given to another member of the group. Individuals who behave in a $34152$ manner are punished by being excluded from the group, and women have a considerable amount of $34153$ in choices regarding work and marriage.

questions.analysis: 答案是“egalitarianism”，因为题干中的“a high level of”对应原文中的“extreme”，而“egalitarianism”是描述狩猎采集者社会的核心特征，符合题干要求的“所有领域的高水平”。

central_sentences.sentence: As the anthropologist Bruce Knauft has remarked, hunter-gatherers are characterised by "extreme political and sexual egalitarianism".
central_sentences.translation: 正如人类学家布鲁斯·克瑙夫特所指出的，狩猎采集者的特点是“极端的政治和性别平等主义”。

syn_ary.synonym: the anthropologist Bruce Knauft has remarked

target_sen.target_sen: As the anthropologist Bruce Knauft has remarked, hunter-gatherers are characterised by "extreme political and sexual egalitarianism".
As the anthropologist Bruce Knauft has remarked, hunter-gatherers are characterised by "extreme political and sexual egalitarianism".
As the anthropologist Bruce Knauft has remarked, hunter-gatherers are characterised by "extreme political and sexual egalitarianism".

questions.analysis: 答案之所以是“status”，是因为题干中的“differences in [ ] occurring”直接对应文章中的“disparities of status don’t arise”。“disparities”与“differences”同义，“don’t arise”与“occurring”同义。通过这些同义替换，可以定位到“status”是正确的答案。

central_sentences.sentence: They also have methods of preserving egalitarianism by ensuring that disparities of status don"t arise
central_sentences.translation: 他们还拥有维持平等的方法，以确保地位差异不会出现。

central_sentences.sentence: The !Kung people of southern Africa, for example, swap arrows before going hunting and when an animal is killed, the acclaim does not go to the person who fired the arrow, but to the person the arrow belongs to.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，南非的洪科人会在狩猎前交换箭，当猎物被杀死时，赞扬不会给予射箭的人，而是给予拥有那支箭的人。

target_sen.target_sen: They also have methods of preserving egalitarianism by ensuring that disparities of status don"t arise.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 "hunting"。这是因为题干中的"credit for one person's success"对应该句中的"the acclaim"（赞誉），并且成功的行为是"before going hunting"（在狩猎前），从而得知题目中的【 】应该填入 "hunting" 来指代成功的活动。这也与题干所描述的内容完全吻合。

central_sentences.sentence: The !Kung people of southern Africa, for example, swap arrows before going hunting and when an animal is killed, the acclaim does not go to the person who fired the arrow, but to the person the arrow belongs to.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，南非的!Kung人会在狩猎前交换箭，当猎物被杀死时，赞誉不会归于射箭的人，而是归于箭的拥有者。

target_sen.target_sen: The !Kung people of southern Africa, for example, swap arrows before going hunting and when an animal is killed, the acclaim does not go to the person who fired the arrow, but to the person the arrow belongs to.

The !Kung people of southern Africa, for example, swap arrows before going hunting and when an animal is killed, the acclaim does not go to the person who fired the arrow, but to the person the arrow belongs to.

syn_ary.synonym: go to the person the arrow belongs to

target_sen.target_sen: The !Kung people of southern Africa, for example, swap arrows before going hunting and when an animal is killed, the acclaim does not go to the person who fired the arrow, but to the person the arrow belongs to.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "excluded from the group" 对应文章中的 "ostracise them, exiling the offender from society"，而 "behave in a 【 】 manner" 对应文章中的 "becomes too domineering"。因此，答案是 "domineering"。这个答案是正确的，因为它准确地反映了文章中描述的行为特征和相应的惩罚措施。

central_sentences.sentence: And if a person becomes too domineering, the other members of the group ostracise them, exiling the offender from society.
central_sentences.translation: 如果一个人变得过于专横，群体中的其他成员会排斥他们，将犯错者驱逐出社会。

syn_ary.synonym: ostracise them, exiling the offender from society

target_sen.target_sen: And if a person becomes too domineering, the other members of the group ostracise them, exiling the offender from society.
And if a person becomes too domineering, the other members of the group ostracise them, exiling the offender from society.
And if a person becomes too domineering, the other members of the group ostracise them, exiling the offender from society.

questions.analysis: 答案“autonomy”出现在原文中，描述了女性在婚姻和工作选择方面的自主权。题干中的“choices regarding work and marriage”与原文中的“select their own marriage partners, decide what work they do”相对应，因此答案是“autonomy”。

central_sentences.sentence: Women in hunter-gatherer groups worldwide often benefit from a high level of autonomy, being able to select their own marriage partners, decide what work they do and work whenever they choose to.
central_sentences.translation: 世界各地的狩猎采集群体中的女性通常享有高度的自主权，能够选择自己的婚姻伴侣，决定自己做什么工作以及何时工作。

target_sen.target_sen: And if a person becomes too domineering, the other members of the group ostracise them, exiling the offender from society.
And if a person becomes too domineering, the other members of the group ostracise them, exiling the offender from society.
Women in hunter-gatherer groups worldwide often benefit from a high level of autonomy, being able to select their own marriage partners, decide what work they do and work whenever they choose to.
Women in hunter-gatherer groups worldwide often benefit from a high level of autonomy, being able to select their own marriage partners, decide what work they do and work whenever they choose to.

groups.desc:
Questions 36-40
Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 36-40 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the views of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the views of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: Some anthropologists are mistaken about the point when the number of societies such as the !Kung began to decline.
questions.analysis: 答案是NOT GIVEN，是因为文章中没有提及任何人类学家关于!Kung社会数量开始减少的具体时间点或他们对此点的错误认识。虽然文章讨论了!Kung社会及相关特质在过去是普遍存在的，并说明了它们可能在人口增长和农业发展时期逐渐发生变化，但并没有评价人类学家对这个转变时间点是否存在误解。

central_sentences.sentence: Many anthropologists believe that societies such as the !Kung were normal until a few thousand years ago, when population growth led to the development of agriculture and a settled lifestyle.
central_sentences.translation: 许多人类学家相信，像!Kung这样的社会在几千年前是正常的，直到人口增长导致农业和定居生活的发展。

target_sen.target_sen: Many anthropologists believe that societies such as the !Kung were normal until a few thousand years ago, when population growth led to the development of agriculture and a settled lifestyle.

syn_ary.synonym: societies such as the !Kung were normal

target_sen.target_sen: Many anthropologists believe that societies such as the !Kung were normal until a few thousand years ago, when population growth led to the development of agriculture and a settled lifestyle.

locating_words.locating_word: the number of societies such as the !Kung began to decline

target_sen.target_sen: In view of the above, there seems little reason to assume that traits such as racism, warfare and male domination should have been selected by evolution - as they would have been of little benefit in the prehistoric era.

questions.title: Humans who developed warlike traits in prehistory would have had an advantage over those who did not.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“warlike traits”在原文中对应“traits such as racism, warfare”，而“advantage”对应“benefit”。原文明确指出，这些特质在史前时代没有什么好处（little benefit），因此不会被进化选择。这与题干中所说的这些特质有优势形成了对立关系，所以答案是NO。

central_sentences.sentence: In view of the above, there seems little reason to assume that traits such as racism, warfare and male domination should have been selected by evolution - as they would have been of little benefit in the prehistoric era.
central_sentences.translation: 综上所述，几乎没有理由假设种族主义、战争和男性统治等特质会被进化选择，因为它们在史前时代几乎没有什么好处。

target_sen.target_sen: In view of the above, there seems little reason to assume that traits such as racism, warfare and male domination should have been selected by evolution - as they would have been of little benefit in the prehistoric era.

In view of the above, there seems little reason to assume that traits such as racism, warfare and male domination should have been selected by evolution - as they would have been of little benefit in the prehistoric era.

questions.title: Being peaceful and cooperative is a natural way for people to behave.
questions.analysis: 题干中的"Being peaceful and cooperative is a natural way for people to behave."意思是“和平与合作是人类的自然行为方式”。在原文中，"traits such as cooperation, egalitarianism, altruism and peacefulness as innate characteristics of human beings"明确指出合作与和平是人类的天生特质，对应题干中的"natural way"。因此，答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: It makes more sense, then, to see traits such as cooperation, egalitarianism, altruism and peacefulness as innate characteristics of human beings.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，将合作、平等主义、利他主义和和平视为人类的天生特质更有意义。

target_sen.target_sen: It makes more sense, then, to see traits such as cooperation, egalitarianism, altruism and peacefulness as innate characteristics of human beings.
It makes more sense, then, to see traits such as cooperation, egalitarianism, altruism and peacefulness as innate characteristics of human beings.
It makes more sense, then, to see traits such as cooperation, egalitarianism, altruism and peacefulness as innate characteristics of human beings.

questions.title: Negative traits are more apparent in some modern cultures than in others.
questions.analysis: 题干问的是负面特质在某些现代文化中是否比在其他文化中更明显（Negative traits are more apparent in some modern cultures than in others）。但是，原文没有直接提到不同现代文化中负面特质的显现程度，也没有进行这样的比较。因此，题干的信息在原文中并没有提到，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: But if prehistoric life wasn"t really as brutal as has often been assumed, why do modern humans behave so selfishly and ruthlessly? Perhaps these negative traits should be seen as a later development, the result of environmental and psychological factors.
central_sentences.translation: 但如果史前生活并不像常常被假设的那样残酷，为什么现代人类会表现得如此自私和残忍？也许这些负面特质应该被看作是后来的发展，是环境和心理因素的结果。

central_sentences.sentence: So, it could well be that the same thing has happened to us. I believe that the end of the hunter-gatherer lifestyle and the advent of farming was connected to a psychological change that occurred in some groups of people.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，这同样的事情很可能也发生在我们身上。我认为猎人-采集者生活方式的终结和农业的出现与某些人群中发生的心理变化有关。

target_sen.target_sen: Perhaps these negative traits should be seen as a later development, the result of environmental and psychological factors.
At any rate, these negative traits appear to have developed so recently that it doesn"t seem feasible to explain them in adaptive or evolutionary terms.

questions.title: Animal research has failed to reveal a link between changes in the environment and the emergence of aggressive tendencies.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“动物研究未能揭示环境变化与攻击性倾向出现之间的联系”，而原文明确指出“研究反复表明，当灵长类动物的自然栖息地受到破坏时，它们往往会变得更加暴力和等级化”。这表明研究确实揭示了环境变化与攻击性倾向之间的联系。因此，题干信息与原文内容相反，正确答案是“NO”。

central_sentences.sentence: Research has shown repeatedly that when the natural habitats of primates such as apes and gorillas are disrupted, they tend to become more violent and hierarchical.
central_sentences.translation: 研究反复表明，当灵长类动物如猿和大猩猩的自然栖息地受到破坏时，它们往往会变得更加暴力和等级化。

target_sen.target_sen: Research has shown repeatedly that when the natural habitats of primates such as apes and gorillas are disrupted, they tend to become more violent and hierarchical.
Research has shown repeatedly that when the natural habitats of primates such as apes and gorillas are disrupted, they tend to become more violent and hierarchical.

syn_ary.synonym: they tend to become more violent

target_sen.target_sen: Research has shown repeatedly that when the natural habitats of primates such as apes and gorillas are disrupted, they tend to become more violent and hierarchical.
